calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by telephone for and with Paula Bibbs (by telephone).
Reisa Elmore appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court advised the Parties that there might be some adjustment of the Trustee's fees and that the amount might lie somewhere in the middle. Ms. Navia stated that Ms. Bibbs has statutory guideline fees at around $50,800.00 that she could have requested, that what Ms. Bibbs has requested is a reduction of almost half and that the request does not include all of the services that were provided by Ms. Bibbs. Ms. Navia also stated that they will have close to a $150,000.00 reserve to address any tax consequences, that an $87,000.00 tax refund was just received last year and that there will be more work for Ms. Bibbs to perform going forward. Ms. Elmore stated that Ms. Navia and Ms. Bibbs have worked diligently, that she greatly appreciates them and that anything the Court can do to expedite this would be appreciated. Ms. Elmore also spoke of her financial struggles due to the pandemic and requested to be allowed some emergency funds. The Court informed Ms. Elmore that her request is not before it. The Second Amended Fourth and Final Account and Report and Supplemental Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Feb-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Paula Bibbs (Trustee).

Ms. Navia addressed the proportionality issue between the Beneficiaries for services provided stating that the real property was specifically bequested to two Beneficiaries, that should there be any fees regarding the real property it should only be allocated to those Beneficiaries, and that those Beneficiaries did not want the responsibility of the property and they wanted the property to be sold. Ms. Navia also stated that the petition was originally filed in February 2019, that there were Probate Examiner's notes that needed to be addressed, that there were administrative continuances by the Court that they had no control of, that Ms. Bibbs spent additional time and that originally Ms. Bibbs fees would have been $50,000.00 and that she is only requesting $25,000.00. The Court stated that it can take the matter under submission, or continue the matter for something to be filed in support of the most recent time. Ms. Navia requested a two-week continuance. The Second Amended Fourth and Final Account and Report and Supplemental Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to February 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Navia is to file a supplemental declaration by February 3, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Paula Bibbs.

The sale was confirmed at $2,600,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $2,610,000.00 is required.

Ms. Navia apologized to the Court for not appearing at the last hearing stating that it was miscalendering on their part. Ms. Navia also requested a continuance stating that sale of the property needs to occur prior to final accounting, that the sale is to be heard on Wednesday and that close of escrow may take a couple of weeks. Status on filing of the fourth account is continued to December 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.
